title: What Are Dog Food Toppers?
content: Pet parents who want to ensure their dogs enjoy their meals while also getting the necessary nutrition are increasingly turning todog food toppers. But what are toppers for dog food, and which dogs truly benefit from them? Here’s what to know.  Dog food toppers add to—not replace—abalanced diet for dogs. They are an easy way to add specific nutrients to their diet or to give regular dog food a flavor boost. However, dog food toppers usually don’t provide complete nutrition themselves. Many types of toppers for dog food are available, including: Tasty sprinkle powders Meaty freeze-dried or air-dried chunks Wet foods Gravies Broths Despite the “topper” name, it’s usually best to thoroughly mix a dog food topper into a dog’s food. Mixing prevents dogs from eating just the topper, leaving their food (and most of its nutritional value) behind in the bowl.  To be clear: Dog food toppers are optional and not a necessary part of a dog’s diet. With that said, they can provide several benefits. Dry dog foodis convenient and usually less expensive than other types of diets, but for some dogs, it’s not all that appealing. Adding a topper can encourage picky dogs to eat. You can rotate through different toppers to add variety at mealtimes without making significant changes to your dog’s diet. Toppers that contain a lot of water, likebroths, are a tasty way to soften kibble. You can even add a water-rich topper to wet dog food to maximize adog’s water intakeand keep them well-hydrated. Some toppers are much higher in protein than typical dog foods. Adding a high-protein topper to dog food is an easy way to up your dog’s protein intake. There are also healthy dog toppers that contain nutraceuticals, or food ingredients typically added to dog food for their health benefits. For example, a topper containingomega-3 fatty acidsmay help reduce inflammation, benefiting dogs with conditions ranging fromallergiestoarthritis.  If you choose to add a topper to your dog’s diet, here are a few tips to help you avoid some of their potential pitfalls. Ahigh-quality, complete, and balanced dog foodstill needs to take center stage in your dog’s diet. No topper can compensate for poor nutrition. Anything new that a dog eats can lead to anupset stomach, somake dietary changesgradually. Initially, add only a small amount of topper to your dog’s food and watch for symptoms, such asvomiting,diarrhea,gassiness, anda poor appetite. Many dog food toppers are labeled for “intermittent or supplemental feeding” because they don’t provide all the nutrition a dog needs. As a general rule, a complete and balanced dog food should supply 90% of a dog’s calories, and the remaining 10% can be from toppers,dog treats, and other extras. Toppers addcalories to your dog’s diet. To preventunwanted weight gain, make sure you cut back on other treats to compensate. Talk to your veterinarian if you have any questions about the best way to incorporate a topper into your dog’s diet. Some dog food toppers require refrigeration, while others do not or should only be refrigerated after you open the package. Follow the storage instructions that are printed on the label of any dog food topper you purchase.  Considering what you’re trying to accomplish is vital to picking out the best dog food toppers for your dog. Here are some vet-recommended options to get you started. For picky eaters:The Honest Kitchen Superfood POUR OVERS Variety Pack Wet Dog Food Topperscome in a variety of flavors that are easy to mix in dry dog food.  Extra moisture:Merrick Chicken Bone Broth Grain-Free Wet Dog Food Topperis 97% moisture, flavored with chicken, sweet potato, apple, parsley, and turmeric.  Protein boost:Blue Buffalo Wilderness Trail Toppers Wild Cuts Chunky Salmon Bites in Hearty Gravy Grain-Free Dog Food Toppercontains 44% protein on a dry matter basis.  Skin and joint support:Marly & Dan Booster Omega-3 Treats & Topper Freeze-Dried Salmon for Dogsis a natural source of anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids.  Joint care:Primalvore Free Range Chicken Bone Broth with Collagen & Turmeric Hip & Joint Support Grain-Free Liquid Dog Food Topperprovides several ingredients known to promote joint health.  Gut health:Instinct Raw Boost Shakers Chicken Recipe Gut Health Grain-Free Freeze-Dried Dog Food Toppercontains probiotics, prebiotics, and other ingredients that support digestion and overall gut health.  Complete nutrition:Solid Gold Complete & Balanced Beef Recipe Air Dried Dog Food Topperprovides complete nutrition, so you can use it to replace more than 10% of your dog’s current diet without the risk of nutrient imbalances. Pet parents typically use dog food toppers to encourage picky eaters to eat more, add variety to a dog’s diet, or supplement particular nutrients. Dog food toppers labeled for “intermittent and supplemental feeding” should make up no more than 10% of a dog’s diet. It’s OK to use a topper at every meal as long as you stay under this amount. Some dog food toppers need to be refrigerated, while others do not or should only be refrigerated after the package is opened. Follow the storage instructions printed on the label of any dog food topper you purchase. WRITTEN BY Veterinarian Dr. Jennifer Coates is an accomplished veterinarian, writer, editor, and consultant with years of experience in the fields of veterinary...
